segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dramatic title card, 'THE DRAMA HAPPENS RIGHT NOW!', accompanied by a voiceover declaring, 'It's an outrageous hour of DNA drama.' A bright, explosive flash reveals '14 OUTRAGEOUS DNA DRAMA' with a 'TV-14' rating. The scene shifts to a studio where a man in a black shirt and a woman in a pink top embrace, while a man in a white shirt with a gold chain looks serious. A female voice exclaims, 'My husband's not the father of your baby!', followed by a male voice asserting, 'That's right! I'm not your baby's...' The host, Maury, asks, 'Why do you think he's denying her?' as a woman in a green top gestures emphatically. Another female voice adds, 'He's so bad! He's denying his own!' The studio audience shouts, 'You're not the father!' A male voice describes the split-screen children, one with curly hair and one with a shaved head, as 'two kids, light brown skin. This baby is Coco Crisp.' Maury then states, 'We're gonna find out right now!' A woman's voice shares, 'At 16 years old, I found out I was pregnant,' with Maury adding, 'And then he said he wasn't the father.' A male voice dismissively says, 'I can't be tied down to a baby that only belonged to me.' Maury questions, 'What are you so upset about?' as a woman in a teal top reacts with shock. Another female voice defensively states, 'I never cheated on...' The atmosphere shifts to celebration as the audience cheers, 'You are the father!', and a man in a white shirt raises his arms triumphantly. The segment concludes with a man in a pinstripe shirt and a woman in a black top with a white collar making peace signs, while Maury sets up the next segment, 'Two kids denied by one man. Today Raymond is here with his...' All throughout, the 'Maury' logo is visible, underscored by suspenseful and dramatic background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a talk show set, immediately establishing a tense paternity drama with the on-screen text '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. A female voice, identified as Tyisha (the Woman in green top), emphatically shouts "Maury, let me break it down to you!" and repeatedly declares "You are the father!" amidst the cheering and shouting of the studio audience. Maury, the host, intervenes, telling Tyisha to "be quiet." Corey, wearing a light-colored shirt with a cross necklace, is seated with his wife Tawanna. He speaks, claiming, "Before y'all jump on her side, she know that I'm a good father. I don't blame her that she want a good father in her life." Tawanna, in a floral top, interjects, stating, "But you don't take care of my daughter!" Corey continues to demand, "Let's get the DNA! Let's get the DNA!" as the audience reacts. Maury announces, "We're gonna find out right now." The on-screen text updates to 'THE RESULTS ARE IN!' as dramatic music builds. Maury addresses Corey, stating, "Now that I know if you're the father, you take care of this child the way you take care of your other one," to which Corey responds, "That's right, I take care of my other one." Tawanna adds, "I take care of her like he take care of his other one." The tension culminates as Maury dramatically declares, "When it comes to two-year-old Sajada, Corey, you are the father!" The bold red text 'YOU ARE THE FATHER!' appears on screen, triggering an explosive reaction from Tyisha (Woman in green top), who leaps up screaming and repeatedly shouts "She crazy!" (or similar), while the studio audience erupts in shock and excitement. The Man in black shirt is shown in a split-screen with two children, the Woman in pink top embraces him, the Woman in teal top expresses shock with an open mouth, the Man in white shirt (celebrating) raises his arms in triumph, and the Man in pinstripe shirt makes a peace sign, all reacting to the dramatic reveal.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens dramatically with Raymond, in a pinstripe shirt with a goatee, and his fiancée Lakiesha, wearing a red top and gold necklace, standing together as on-screen text highlights Raymond’s claim that he is not the father of Shanae’s children. Lakiesha immediately asserts in a firm voice, “That's me. Right here. Not on me. He is not the father,” as the scene cuts to Maury, in a black sweater, gesturing emphatically on stage with the show’s logo and graphic overlay '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Maury then states, “They're determined to prove that Shanae is wrong, wrong,” as Raymond adds in his own voice, “Shanae, you know as well as I know that these kids are not mine.” The atmosphere is intense, with rapid cuts to black-and-white and sepia-toned close-ups of a wide-eyed baby, symbolizing the emotional stakes. A woman with short hair and large hoop earrings, wearing a black and white patterned top, appears, speaking with a concerned and aggressive expression, accusing Shanae, “What kind of woman has Raymond wait 15 hours for your kids when you don't even show? That makes you a nasty, manipulative bitch.” Raymond continues his defense, “How dare you give Raymond Jr. my name? He's not even my blood. And you tricked me into signing that birth certificate just so you could get child support off of me.” The woman with short hair interjects again, “Raymond Jr. has red hair. He's not even as dark as my man. Don't you dare say that this is none of my business,” adding, “Any whore that takes advantage of my man becomes my business.” Raymond then questions the paternity of another child, “And as for Phoenix, we weren't even together when you got pregnant. So how do I know she's mine?” The woman with short hair delivers a final scathing remark, “You are nothing but a lying, childish, poor excuse for a mother. Quit calling and leave my man alone,” prompting Maury to react with “How about that?” and “What do you think?” The segment uses quick transitions, split screens, and dramatic zooms, with alternating warm red and stark monochrome lighting, heightening the tension around the DNA test results and the couple’s relationship under scrutiny, all underscored by audience reactions and dramatic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Maury, wearing a black sweater, standing on stage with expressive hand gestures, driving the narrative with intensity, as a graphic overlay behind him reads '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Maury's voice questions, "Why aren't you the father of that little girl?", to which Raymond, in a pinstripe shirt with a goatee, defensively replies, "Because I wasn't around." Lakiesha, in a red top and gold necklace, interjects, "You were living together... We were living together in Vegas... when I got pregnant with my daughter," as Raymond insists, "I was not around." The scene cuts to a split-screen showing a man in a black shirt embracing a woman in a pink top, then to a woman in a black top with a white collar, her expression serious. Maury asks Raymond if he'll take care of the kids if he's the father, to which Raymond responds, "If I get to see him," and "She can keep him" if he doesn't, while Lakiesha asserts, "No, he's not... If I don't get to see him, he has no say." Lakiesha then clarifies, "If he was man enough to come around without her," drawing applause and cheers from the studio audience, who are visible reacting with shock and engagement. A close-up of a baby’s wide-eyed, innocent face is displayed, anchoring the emotional stakes. The audience’s reactions intensify with building dramatic music as the graphic 'THE RESULTS ARE IN!' flashes. Maury holds up a yellow envelope labeled 'DDC,' and the camera cuts to Raymond, who looks down, visibly distressed. Maury dramatically declares, "When it comes to one and a half year old Phoenix, Raymond, you are the father!", triggering loud cheers, gasps, and applause from the audience. The graphic 'YOU ARE THE FATHER!' appears, and Raymond’s expression shifts to one of shock and disbelief, while Lakiesha looks on with a mix of concern and resignation, demanding, "Can I get an apology?" The studio lighting remains warm and dramatic, emphasizing the tension and emotional weight of the moment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a tense, dramatically lit studio with red and orange hues, where Michael, in a blue and white striped shirt, sits with arms crossed, displaying a stern, defensive expression. An on-screen graphic declares '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' setting the stage for a paternity drama. The camera cuts to Anna, in a pink top, her face contorted in distress and accusation as she emotionally states, "I'm tired of him promising to come see the baby. I have kept the baby up late past bedtime, waiting for him to come see him... He promises to come see him, he shows up at 1 o'clock in the morning. What 9-month-old's gonna be awake at 1 o'clock in the morning?" A lower-third graphic identifies her, stating 'ANNA SAYS THAT MICHAEL REFUSES TO DO ANYTHING FOR HER OR THEIR BABY.' She continues, her voice rising in frustration, "I even let my baby go stay at a family member of his house, so he could spend the night with his father. He didn't even come home! My baby was there for two days and he never come home to see him. He stayed at a different family member's house." Maury, the host, in a black sweater, gestures emphatically as he interjects, "Because he says you were cheating." Anna, with tearful eyes, vehemently denies, "I never cheated on him. Never in my life did I ever cheat on him!" prompting loud cheers and applause from the visible studio audience. The camera alternates between close-ups of her emotional intensity and Michael’s unyielding gaze, with a lower-third graphic reinforcing his denial: 'SAYS ANNA SLEPT WITH ANOTHER MAN THE NIGHT SHE CONCEIVED.' Maury then asks, "You're positive he's the father," to which Anna emphatically responds, "I am a million percent sure he is the father," a claim also highlighted by a lower-third graphic. Brief cuts show a calm baby in a blue bouncer sucking on a toy, and a photo of a newborn in a hospital setting with medical tubes, underscoring the emotional stakes. Maury probes further, "So, I mean, when did all this start? You all were together, right? You were a couple?" Anna confirms, "We were together. He even asked me to marry him." When Maury asks why Michael would think she was cheating, Anna explains, "Because he had friends and family members trying to convince him that I was, and all I want to do is trap him with this baby." Maury responds with a slightly sarcastic, "Oh, is that what it was? I see," before being cut off. The segment ends with Anna’s face in close-up, her eyes wide with pain, as her emotional plea lingers.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Maury, wearing a black sweater, standing on stage with expressive hand gestures, introducing a dramatic paternity case under the graphic '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. He calls out, "Here's Michael. Michael, come on out." The scene cuts to Michael, a man in a blue and white striped shirt, walking into the studio with a serious expression. He is immediately confronted by Anna, a woman in a pink top, who appears distressed and tearful, accusing him with intense emotion, stating, "because our baby needs both of us. He needs his father as well as me." Anna gestures emphatically, pointing at Michael while passionately shouting, "I cheated on you? I cheated on you? Talking about he cheated on you? Three different times? Get out! I never cheated on you! Look at that baby! Look at that baby! Look at that baby! That is your baby! He needed you and you weren't there for him!" She continues to challenge him, asking, "When have you ever been there for him? His family has been there for him more than you have!" Michael, with a stern and defensive expression, stands with arms crossed, responding, "I have been there for him the whole time." Anna presses him further, "Where were you his first doctor's appointment? Where were you at when he screams daddy, daddy, daddy? Where?" Maury intervenes, urging, "Let him answer. Let him answer." Anna dismissively states, "Nowhere." Maury then addresses Michael, saying, "She says your family members and friends are convincing you of this." Michael, shown in close-up with a pensive look, reveals, "Well, I walked into her apartment and she was with, let him talk, with another guy," eliciting gasps and "Oohs!" from the studio audience. Anna immediately counters, "When were you? When were you?" A lower-third graphic identifies Michael and states he believes he is not the father. The camera alternates between close-ups of Anna’s emotional outburst and Michael’s stoic reactions, capturing the tension. The studio audience is visible in the background, some with shocked expressions, adding to the drama. A photo of a newborn baby in a hospital setting, wearing a blue and white striped hat with medical tubes, is displayed on screen, emphasizing the stakes. Maury sets up the impending reveal, "Well, if you are the father, you'll be in that child's life, right?" Michael responds, "Oh, yes, that's why she's at my house right now." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman in a purple top, identified as Jessica, speaking with a serious expression, her braided hair and large hoop earrings visible, as the on-screen graphic reads '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. The host, Maury, wearing a black sweater, is heard introducing the segment, stating he "names her for putting so much doubt in Ernest's heart," and inviting viewers to "listen to what Ernest and Janette had to say." A woman's emphatic voice, identified as Janette, immediately declares, "Jessica, I'm a trillion percent sure that my brother is not your son's father." The scene cuts to a man in a black shirt, shown in a split-screen with two children, suggesting he is a central figure in the paternity storyline. A man's angry voice, identified as Ernest, then accuses Jessica, stating, "Jessica, when I let you move in with me, I know you were sleeping with one of my friends. And I know one of them might be the father." The video then transitions to a man in a white shirt with a gold chain, his face partially shadowed, appearing intense and serious, as Ernest's voice continues, "Back in the days, I didn't think how dirty you was 'cause I was in love with you. But when I found out you had four kids with four different fathers, I knew you was a ho." A woman in a green top is shown with a distressed expression, speaking emphatically, later holding a baby, with dramatic lighting shifts, while Janette's voice returns, asserting, "I'm going to protect my brother from a nasty, trifling bitch like you." Ernest's voice continues his accusations, "You stay out until 5 o'clock in the morning. And I know you was creeping 'cause that's what hoes do." The scene shifts to a man in a white shirt walking down a ramp into the studio, appearing serious and focused, as Janette's voice declares, "Today is the day that we find the truth, and when we do, you better back off." Ernest's voice then demands, "I want you to change his name, leave my family the hell alone, and go find your real baby father." The studio audience is shown reacting with a collective "Oh!" and then applause, adding to the emotional weight. Maury is then heard asking, "What do you think of that?" A woman's defensive and agitated voice, identified as Jessica, shouts, "She wasn't there when I slept with him. She was not there. I'm a hundred percent sure that he's not the father! And what, what did you say? What? What is it? What is it?" The segment concludes with the intense verbal exchange.
